metadata.title: Why an agent's memory guardrail is one the model can't see
metadata.purpose: Tools are model-controllable (the model decides when to invoke, result returns mid-turn); hooks are SDK-controllable (fire on lifecycle events, invisible, can prepend context before the model sees the prompt). Deliberate saving needs the model's agency; guaranteed injection must not depend on it.
metadata.clock: narration (Kokoro af_kore) — master clock; conform each beat to measured audio
metadata.still_lanes: c2v (two-lane diagram, write in tool lane, read in hook lane), raster (hook-event table)
metadata.prerequisites: what tools are, basic agent loop
metadata.exclusions: all hook event types in depth, PreToolUse/PostToolUse guardrail patterns, raw hook code
metadata.visual_object: Two lanes — Tool lane (model emits → SDK runs → result returns) vs. Hook lane (event fires → callback runs → context silently prepended) — memory's write path in one, read path in the other.
metadata.hook: Two mechanisms intercept what an agent does; the model knows only one of them exists.

beats.narration_text: Stage-3 memory uses both at once — a `save_memory` tool the model chooses to call, and a `UserPromptSubmit` hook that silently injects saved context before every turn without the model's knowledge.
beats.scaffold_note: from Key case — cold open on the concrete instance

beats.narration_text: One task (save memories, then re-inject them) should predict one mechanism; here it's split across two entirely different primitives — why does writing memory need a tool and reading it need a hook?
Two mechanisms intercept what an agent does; the model knows only one of them exists.

Tools are model-controllable (the model decides when to invoke, result returns mid-turn); hooks are SDK-controllable (fire on lifecycle events, invisible, can prepend context before the model sees the prompt). Deliberate saving needs the model's agency; guaranteed injection must not depend on it.
beats.visual: Two lanes — Tool lane (model emits → SDK runs → result returns) vs. Hook lane (event fires → callback runs → context silently prepended) — memory's write path in one, read path in the other.
beats.scaffold_note: the resolving mechanism; orbits the visual object

beats.narration_text: Model saves "user prefers bullet points" via the tool; next session, before the user types, the hook reads the store and prepends the preference; the model answers in bullets — told at session start, not remembering.

Tools are model-controllable (the model decides when to invoke, result returns mid-turn); hooks are SDK-controllable (fire on lifecycle events, invisible, can prepend context before the model sees the prompt). Deliberate saving needs the model's agency; guaranteed injection must not depend on it.
